codesAndRewards.title: Bee Swarm Simulator Codes and Rewards
codesAndRewards.intro: This module separates active reward codes, expired codes, group-related rewards, and DLC code status so you can claim real rewards in Bee Swarm Simulator. Join the official Roblox club first, then redeem codes from the in-game settings menu.

activeCodes.reward: 1 Marshmallow Bee, 4 Strawberries, 4 Sunflower Seeds, 4 Blueberries, 4 Pineapples, 4 Micro-Converters, 4 Field Dice, 4 Gumdrops, 4 Jelly Beans, 4 Stingers
15 Gumdrops, 1 Marshmallow Bee, 15 Sunflower Seeds, 15 Strawberries, 15 Pineapples, 15 Blueberries, 15 Bitterberries, 1 Red Balloon

redeemSteps: Join the Bee Swarm Simulator Club on Roblox.
Launch the official Bee Swarm Simulator experience.
Open the cog icon in the top-left settings area.
Enter the code in the Codes box and press Redeem.
Use field boost codes only when you are ready to farm, because boost rewards activate immediately.
codesAndRewards.dlcNote: The official DLC page is the place to check Bee Swarm Simulator DLC redemption status. Treat any other site claiming to redeem DLC codes as fake.

bssBeginnerGuide.title: Bee Swarm Simulator Beginner Guide
bssBeginnerGuide.intro: Bee Swarm Simulator is built around a repeating climb: gather pollen in fields, make honey at your hive, grow your swarm, complete bear quests, unlock higher areas, and use stronger bees to fight bugs and monsters. This beginner path keeps the first session focused on useful progress instead of random spending.

steps.title: Join the Official Experience
steps.description: Start from the correct Roblox game page by Onett. Claim an empty hive when you spawn, then stay near the early fields until your first bag upgrades are done.
steps.tip: Use the official game page and official club link for rewards and updates.

steps.title: Collect Pollen First
steps.description: Walk into a field and use your collector tool, let your bees gather pollen with you, and pick up the ability tokens they drop.
steps.tip: Early progress comes from short collect-and-convert loops, not from rushing into far areas.

steps.title: Convert Pollen Into Honey
steps.description: Return to your hive when your bag is full and stand near it to convert pollen into honey, then spend early honey on practical upgrades.
steps.tip: When your bag fills too quickly, prioritize container upgrades. When collection feels slow, prioritize tool upgrades.

steps.title: Hatch More Bees
steps.description: Buy eggs when you can afford them, place them into empty hive slots, and keep useful rare, epic, legendary, and event bees as your hive improves.
steps.tip: More bees means more gathering, more combat help, and access to higher mountain zones.

steps.title: Follow Bear Quests
steps.description: Complete Black Bear-style pollen quests for steady rewards, use Mother Bear-style tasks to learn bee leveling and treats, and take combat quests once your bees can handle bugs safely.
steps.tip: Quests point you toward fields, mechanics, and rewards in a cleaner order than pure guessing.

steps.title: Unlock New Areas Gradually
steps.description: Increase your bee count to pass gates, defeat bugs and monsters for drops when they are manageable, and look for hidden treasures and useful dispensers as new areas open.
steps.tip: Do not rush expensive upgrades before your hive, quests, and bag capacity can actually support them.

quickTips: Always start from the official Bee Swarm Simulator game page to avoid fake copies.
Keep your bees fed with treats so they level up and gather faster.
Save high-value materials instead of spending every item the moment it drops.
Use field boost codes only during active farming sessions for the best return.

progressionAndGearOrder.title: Bee Swarm Simulator Progression and Gear Order
progressionAndGearOrder.intro: Progression in Bee Swarm Simulator is about keeping your hive count, collector strength, container size, quests, badges, materials, and amulets moving together. This path gives you a practical order for what to improve next across early game, mid game, and late game.

tasks: Claim a hive and collect pollen in early fields.
Convert pollen into honey at your hive.

stages.goal: Increase bee count and buy practical shop upgrades as new areas open.
priorities: Better collectors when pollen gathering feels slow
Better bags when your container fills too fast
More hive slots when new gates require more bees
tasks: Use quests to choose fields instead of farming randomly.
Fight manageable bugs for drops and combat practice.
Collect badges naturally while farming fields.

stages.goal: Prepare for stronger farming, better questing, and material-heavy upgrades.
priorities: Sprinkler upgrades as farming sessions get longer
tasks: Improve hive variety for gifted bonuses and quest coverage.
Work toward stronger masks and guards.
Build a material stockpile instead of spending every item instantly.

stages.goal: Move into crafted gear, event bee planning, amulets, and Spirit Bear progression.
priorities: Honey Mask, Bubble Mask, or Fire Mask based on farming direction
Honeycomb Belt and Petal Wand or Petal Belt progression
tasks: Complete Spirit Bear quests for Spirit Petals.
Level key combat bees for stronger boss and bug fights.

stages.goal: Specialize the hive and chase high-cost late-game upgrades.
priorities: Diamond Mask, Demon Mask, or Gummy Mask based on hive direction
Supreme Star Amulet and endgame collector path
tasks: Specialize into blue, red, or white hive planning.
Use boosts when field, nectar, balloon, gumdrop, and amulet setup are ready.

beeTierList.title: Bee Swarm Simulator Bee Tier List
beeTierList.intro: Bee value changes as your hive grows. Early players need collection, focus, token support, and simple damage. Mid-game players need quest coverage, marks, baby love, music buffs, and event bee value. Late-game players rank bees by hive color, boost setup, combat needs, and amulet synergy.

tiers.why: Strong early value from scaling damage, instant collection, focus and melody support, baby love, and field marks.
Good early utility through focus, blue or red collection support, haste, attack help, and general quest coverage.
Usable early bees that fill roles while the hive is small, though many get replaced by stronger support and gifted bonuses.
Starter filler bees. Gifted Basic Bee is valuable later for its hive bonus, but normal starters should not block stronger upgrades.
High-impact bees for combat, goo, marks, melody, baby love, and long-term account value.
Strong bees that grow in importance as the hive moves toward color direction, better boosts, and stronger farming routes.
Useful role fillers for damage, color support, focus, and quests, but less central than the main support and mythic bees.
Blue hives lean on balloons, bubbles, frogs, melody support, pollination, and advanced late-game utility.
Red hives value target marks, flames, precision, strong combat, melody support, and late-game digital utility.
White hives value marks, goo, pollination, conversion support, and strong scaling from advanced hive tools.
Tabby scales extremely well, Photon gives strong instant collection, Vicious improves combat, and Gummy supports goo-heavy farming.
Crimson and Cobalt support red and blue collection, Festive adds useful tokens, and Windy becomes powerful once its unlock path is available.
Puppy helps when leveling bees matters heavily, Bear Bee depends on Robux access, and Digital Bee belongs to advanced late-game progression.

hiveBuildsAndColors.title: Bee Swarm Simulator Hive Builds and Colors
hiveBuildsAndColors.intro: Bee Swarm Simulator starts as a mixed-hive progression game: you collect pollen, convert honey, complete bear quests, unlock new zones, and discover bees with different traits. Color-specialized hives become stronger later, but switching too early can slow quests, badges, and resource farming.

builds.playstyle: Flexible questing across red, blue, and white pollen tasks
coreBees: A balanced mix of red, blue, white, utility, and gifted bees
Event bees with strong general utility
Bees that help with tokens, focus, haste, melody, and baby love
gearFocus: Quest-friendly masks and mid-game shop gear
Do not overcommit to one color before major questlines and amulet progress
builds.bestFor: Players still completing bear quests, unlocking gates, building gifted bee bonuses, and collecting broad resources.

builds.stage: Late game and endgame farming path
builds.playstyle: Long farming sessions, high capacity, balloon-style storage, and steady honey gains
gearFocus: Tide Popper path for advanced blue progression
builds.bestFor: Players who prefer longer farming sessions, capacity scaling, and blue-heavy routes such as Pine Tree Forest.

builds.stage: Late game and endgame active boost path
builds.playstyle: High burst farming, active boosting, flame and precision gameplay, and stronger combat
gearFocus: Dark Scythe path for advanced red progression
builds.bestFor: Players who enjoy active boosts, red-field farming such as Pepper Patch or Rose Field, and higher interaction during sessions.

builds.playstyle: High-investment farming built around goo, marks, precision, and strong white pollen scaling
gearFocus: Gummyballer path for advanced white progression
builds.bestFor: Players with deep resources, strong amulets, strong gifted bonuses, and enough gear progress to support goo-heavy farming.

questsAndBearsGuide.title: Bee Swarm Simulator Quests and Bears Guide
questsAndBearsGuide.intro: Bee Swarm Simulator uses bear quests as the backbone of progression. You collect pollen, defeat enemies, gather tokens, unlock zones, and earn rewards that push the hive forward instead of wandering the mountain aimlessly.

bears.name: Black Bear
bears.role: Core pollen questline and early progression guide
focus: Progress from early areas into wider map goals

bears.name: Brown Bear
bears.role: Repeatable quest cycle for steady rewards

bears.name: Mother Bear
bears.role: Bee care, treats, bonding, and basic hive improvement

bears.name: Science Bear
bears.role: Technology, crafting, translators, and advanced mechanics

bears.name: Spirit Bear
bears.role: Spirit Petal route and advanced progression
focus: Large pollen goals and field variety

bears.name: Robo Bear
bears.role: Robo Bear Challenge, cogs, upgrades, and mechanical enemies

bears.name: Event Bears
bears.role: Beesmas-style quests, presents, ornaments, and seasonal catalogs

gamepassesAndStoreGuide.title: Bee Swarm Simulator Gamepasses and Store Guide
gamepassesAndStoreGuide.intro: Bee Swarm Simulator has Roblox store content, in-game purchase routes, official merch, toys, and a dedicated DLC code page. The main decision is whether a purchase helps progression, saves time, adds convenience, or only belongs in the physical merch bucket.

items.name: Bear Bee
items.what: Grants access to Bear Bee, an event bee known for bear morph tokens that boost pollen and movement during its effect.
items.bestFor: Players who want a permanent bee upgrade with broad farming value.

items.name: x2 Convert Speed
items.what: Makes bees convert pollen into honey at the hive faster.
items.bestFor: Players who fill bags quickly or spend too much time converting at the hive.

items.name: x2 Bee Gather
items.what: Improves pollen gathered directly by bees from flowers.
items.bestFor: Players with enough bee activity and field time to benefit from stronger direct gathering.

items.name: Cub Buddy
items.what: Adds a small bear companion that follows you, collects tokens, and can grant gifts.
items.bestFor: Players who farm actively and want help collecting tokens.

items.name: Tickets, Eggs, and Consumable Packs
items.what: Provides resources such as tickets, eggs, Royal Jelly-style rerolls, boost materials, or event-limited bundles.
items.bestFor: Players who understand exactly which bottleneck they are paying to skip.

items.name: Official DLC Codes Page
items.what: The official DLC page is the place to check Bee Swarm Simulator DLC redemption status.
items.bestFor: Players checking whether toy or DLC-related codes can be redeemed.

items.name: Official Toys
items.what: Includes Brown Bear, Panda Bear, Gummy Bear, Mother Bear, and Black Bear action figure packs, plus Mystery Bee and honeycomb case products.
items.bestFor: Players buying physical Bee Swarm Simulator products rather than in-game power.

items.name: Official Apparel and Plush Items
items.what: Includes plush bees, shirts, hoodies, and other Bee Swarm Simulator branded products.
items.bestFor: Fans who want official Bee Swarm Simulator items outside Roblox.

beesmasEventsAndTrading.title: Bee Swarm Simulator Beesmas Events and Trading
beesmasEventsAndTrading.intro: Bee Swarm Simulator events often add temporary quests, map decorations, presents, ornaments, Beequips, stickers, trading goals, and limited-time boosts. Treat event rewards as seasonal routes and use official channels first for active event details.

events.name: Bee Swarm Winter Event
events.action: Complete event bear quests while the winter event is active.

events.name: Bee Bear and Event Quest Routes
events.action: Prioritize event quests before normal grinding when the seasonal route is live.

events.name: x2 Pollen and Convert Rate Event
rewards: Best value during active farming sessions
events.action: Use field boosts, consumables, and active farming time during the event window.

events.name: Cub Buddy and Sticker Trading Goals
content: Work through Bee Bear-related event content
events.action: Trade carefully, confirm item names before accepting, and avoid off-platform item sellers.

events.name: Stickers and Hive Collection
events.action: Keep useful stickers for quests and trades instead of discarding everything.

events.name: Onett and Bee Swarm Community Updates
events.action: Check official Roblox, Onett, Bee Swarm shop, and Discord sources before trusting code lists or marketplace claims.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Bee Swarm Simulator

questions.question: What is Bee Swarm Simulator?
questions.answer: Bee Swarm Simulator is a Roblox game by Onett where you grow a swarm of bees, collect pollen, convert it into honey, hatch new bees, and complete bear quests to climb the mountain and unlock new zones.

questions.question: How do I redeem Bee Swarm Simulator codes?
questions.answer: Join the Bee Swarm Simulator Club on Roblox, launch the game, open the cog settings icon in the top-left, enter a code in the Codes box, and press Redeem. Field boost codes activate immediately, so use them right before farming.

questions.question: Where can I play Bee Swarm Simulator?
questions.answer: Bee Swarm Simulator is free to play on Roblox across PC, Mac, mobile, and Xbox. Always launch it from the official Roblox game page to avoid fake copies.

questions.question: What is the main goal in Bee Swarm Simulator?
questions.answer: The goal in Bee Swarm Simulator is to grow your hive, collect pollen, make honey, complete bear quests, unlock new zones, and build a stronger swarm of bees all the way to a full 50-bee endgame hive.

questions.question: How many bees can a Bee Swarm Simulator hive hold?
questions.answer: A full Bee Swarm Simulator hive holds up to 50 bees. New hive slots unlock as you progress, and higher bee counts open new gates, fields, and mountain zones.

questions.question: Are Bee Swarm Simulator event rewards permanent?
questions.answer: Most Bee Swarm Simulator event rewards, such as Beesmas presents and seasonal quests, are time-limited. Complete event routes while they are live and check official channels for active event details.
